FastVisa

FastVisa is a company that specializes in immigration practice management and legal intake software within the legal technology sector. The company provides workflow automation and case management software to immigration law firms, including customizable forms, a billing system, a client intake system, and tools for managing case-related communications and payments. The focus is on the legal services industry, particularly in immigration law. It was founded in 2016 and is based in Dallas, Texas.

W

WayLit [...] ## Compare Ellis to Competitors

C

Casehopper

Casehopper focuses on the immigration process through artificial intelligence, operating within the legal technology industry. The company offers artificial intelligence (AI) powered tools for personalized attorney matching, visa application assistance, and form-filling services. Its primary clientele includes individuals seeking immigration assistance and immigration attorneys looking for technological efficiency. It was founded in 2023 and is based in Dover, Delaware.

results.title: Ali Ellis - Manhattan Alternative
results.content:
Ali Ellis (she/they), ATR-BC, LCAT, is an art therapist who works with individuals, couples, and families exploring identity, gender, relationships, and life transitions. Informed by family and systems training, their approach integrates attachment and trauma-informed perspectives to understand how relational patterns develop and shift over time. Ali has experience supporting neurodivergent clients, sexual assault survivors, and people in diverse relationship structures, including polyamory and non-monogamy. She prioritizes building a steady, trusting therapeutic relationship where clients can move at their own pace. Through art therapy and relational, strengths-based work, Ali helps clients reconnect with creativity, resilience, and a stronger sense of agency and self-understanding. # Ellis

#### At a glance

Updated on Jun 1, 2026

In this article

 Ellis name popularity
 Popularity over time
 Names similar to Ellis
 Baby name lists including Ellis

## Ellisname meaning and origin

This description was written by AI. Keep in mind, AI can make mistakes.

Ellis is an elegant English name meaning "The Lord is my God." It’s an Anglicized form of the Welsh name Elisedd and has been embraced both as a first name and a surname throughout history, carrying a sense of kindness with its meaning "benevolent." [...] Ellis also holds patriotic significance, famously linked to Ellis Island in New York—a historic immigration station known as the gateway for millions of newcomers to the U.S. Its Welsh and English roots give it a timeless, rich heritage, explored alongside other classic options in our old-fashioned names due for a revival article.

results.title: Ellis Island Immigration Depot Opens | History | Research Starters | EBSCO Research
results.content: Congressional leaders also concluded that the federal government should construct a new immigration center. A joint committee was appointed to suggest a desirable location. The committee soon decided that the most secure location would be one of the government-owned islands in New York harbor. Their first choice was Bedloe’s Island, where the Statue of Liberty had been built four years earlier, but the suggestion aroused a storm of protests. Led by Senator McPherson of New Jersey, the committee then proposed Ellis Island, even though it would have to be greatly enlarged to accommodate the expected number of immigrants. New Yorkers, eager to eliminate the dangerous munitions dump still housed on the island, strongly supported the recommendation. [...] Congressional leaders also concluded that the federal government should construct a new immigration center. A joint committee was appointed to suggest a desirable location. The committee soon decided that the most secure location would be one of the government-owned islands in New York harbor. Their first choice was Bedloe’s Island, where the Statue of Liberty had been built four years earlier, but the suggestion aroused a storm of protests. Led by Senator McPherson of New Jersey, the committee then proposed Ellis Island, even though it would have to be greatly enlarged to accommodate the expected number of immigrants. New Yorkers, eager to eliminate the dangerous munitions dump still housed on the island, strongly supported the recommendation. [...] Ellis Island, which now covers a land area of about 27.5 acres, has been known by a variety of names during its history. Early Dutch settlers called it Oyster Island, because they used the site for shucking oysters. When the British took possession of New Amsterdam, changing its name to New York, they called the island Gibbet Island, indicating that it was a place for executing pirates. Purchased by a businessman named Samuel Ellis late in the eighteenth century, the island took the name of its new owner. In 1808, Ellis’s heirs sold the island to the federal government for $10,000. For a few years, it again served as a major execution post, but just before the U.S. Civil War it become a military arsenal. After the war, the future use of the island became the subject of heated political
